title: mattiastrum cristatum subsp. carduchorum
intro: Mattiastrum cristatum subsp. carduchorum, also known as Carduchorum Mattiastrum, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae, native to Iran. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits of promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting infections. Herbal preparations of Mattiastrum cristatum subsp. carduchorum include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
